key_insight: An educational proof assistant can improve students’ proof construction and make their reasoning more explicit, with benefits that transfer to handwritten proofs.
engineering_relevance: For theorem-proving and math-education tools, this suggests controlled natural language assistants can support skill transfer even across language and classroom heterogeneity.
method: Quasi-experiment in an introductory proof course
Mixed setting: multiple teachers, study programs, and Dutch-English context
Used in-class observation, surveys, and student proof/performance analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports observational and performance data suggesting carryover to pen-and-paper proofs and higher grades in the Mathematics-Computer Science program.
watch_item: Results are suggestive rather than causal because students self-selected into using Waterproof instead of being randomly assigned.
paper_title: The Educational Proof Assistant Waterproof in an Introductory Proof Course: Proof Construction and Learning Processes
